description: Model-specific repair rules for the xiaomi/mimo family (MiMo-V2.5, MiMo-V2.5-Pro). MiMo is a budget automation model with ~94% cache hit rate — used heavily for script-driven cron jobs and monitoring. It makes more frequent tool-call formatting errors than frontier models but benefits disproportionately from targeted repairs because its prompts are highly repetitive (high cache → cheap to fix). Known failure modes: string-number coercion on script output parameters, parameter name confusion with positional args, and the universal 'Big Four' (handled by plugin core).

repairs._comment: MiMo frequently sends 'cmd' when tools expect 'command' (shell tool calls)
MiMo uses 'msg' when tools expect 'message' (messaging tools)
MiMo sends 'path_to_file' when tools expect 'path' or 'file_path'
MiMo sends 'prompt' when tools expect 'query' (search tools)
MiMo string-number coercion — frequent due to script output being all strings
MiMo sometimes sends 'content' when tools expect 'text' (TTS, messaging)
MiMo sends 'delay' when tools expect 'timeout' or 'duration_ms'
